verdict_reasoning: VEGFA is a secreted dimeric growth factor with an N-terminal signal peptide but no transmembrane domain in any isoform. The diffusible isoforms (VEGF121) are freely soluble; the longer isoforms (VEGF165, VEGF189) associate with cell-surface and ECM heparan sulfate proteoglycans post-secretion, but this interaction is reversible and fully releasable by heparin or heparinase — not wash-resistant or covalently tethered. No isoform is a TM-anchored precursor. All clinical programs (bevacizumab, ranibizumab, aflibercept) target the circulating/secreted pool, not a cell-surface protein body. Pre-no checklist: (1) no cell-surface-directed therapeutic targeting the VEGFA protein body on the outer leaflet; (2) not a TM-precursor/shedding substrate; (3) HSPG binding is reversible equilibrium — stable_surface_attachment excluded; (4) no documented PM minority pool with own anchor; (5) no gene-specific evidence for cancer-state, cell-death, activation-induced, or germline-restricted outer-leaflet ecto-pool. Verdict: secreted_only.
